name: Sericia — Dify KB sync (mirror Payload articles to chatbot knowledge base)
_purpose: Every published article (static journal + Payload pSEO) should land in the Dify chatbot's knowledge base so /api/dify-chat answers are grounded in the same content visitors read on /journal. Runs daily at 03:00 JST when DeepSeek drain has settled and traffic is lowest.
_decision_journal: Why Dify KB and not vector-RAG-from-Postgres-direct: Dify already runs a managed embeddings pipeline + similarity search + retrieval-augmented prompts behind the chat API; reimplementing that is two weeks of work for zero gain. We push markdown via the documents/create endpoint and let Dify handle chunking + embeddings.

nodes.name: Daily 03:00 JST
nodes.notes: 03:00 JST = 18:00 UTC. After the pseo-generate hourly cron has drained the day's batch and well before the morning Asia traffic window.

nodes.name: POST /api/dify-kb-sync
nodes.notes: since_hours=26 — slight overlap with the previous run guards against missed articles if a generation lands on the boundary. max_documents=100 caps a runaway batch that could exhaust Dify rate limits. The endpoint itself is idempotent — re-pushing an unchanged article is a no-op.

nodes.name: Slack: KB sync summary
nodes.notes: Quiet success path. The sync runs nightly so a daily Slack ping is fine; if the volume becomes annoying we can flip the cron to weekly summary.

nodes.name: Slack: nothing to sync
nodes.notes: Optional — comment out if the daily heartbeat becomes noise. Useful early on as proof the cron is alive.
